title: Maple-Glazed Salmon
description: A simple three-ingredient glaze of maple syrup, bourbon, and Dijon reduced into a syrupy finish for cedar plank salmon. Also excellent on pork chops.

ingredients.name: dried thyme or Herbs de Provence
cedar plank, soaked in water at least 1 hour

instructions.text: Soak cedar plank in water for at least 1 hour before grilling.

Combine maple syrup, bourbon, Dijon mustard, thyme or Herbs de Provence, and liquid smoke in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer about 30 minutes until reduced and syrupy. Set aside.
Preheat grill to medium-high. Place soaked cedar plank on grill over direct heat, lid closed, until it starts smoking, about 3–4 minutes. Move to indirect heat.
Place salmon fillets skin-side down on the smoking plank. Close lid and cook 10–15 minutes until salmon is just cooked through.
Spoon glaze generously over salmon and serve immediately. Pass extra glaze at the table.

notes:
The glaze also works beautifully on pork chops -- brush on during the last few minutes of grilling.

Liquid smoke is listed as 1/2 tsp but the original card just says 'smoke' -- adjust to taste depending on how smoky you want it.

The glaze keeps refrigerated for 2 weeks and is worth making a double batch.
